summary: Validate privacy/confidential miniapps + audit anchor staking/reward logic + oracle service availability/integration quality
logs: [audit:services] failed: API Error: The socket connection was closed unexpectedly. For more information, pass `verbose: true` in the second argument to fetch()

confidential_apps.how: encrypt to oracle key, decrypt in-TEE
encrypt to oracle key, reveal in-TEE

audits.evidence:
CRYPTO CORRECTNESS — VERIFIED MATCH. Three client/server crypto impls agree byte-for-byte: platform apps/shared/utils/morpheus-confidential-envelope.ts (used by neo-message + private-transfer), oracle apps/web/lib/browser-encryption.ts, and worker workers/nitro-worker/src/oracle/crypto.js. All pin ENVELOPE_VERSION=2, alg=X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM, ENVELOPE_INFO='morpheus-confidential-payload-v2', HKDF info = INFO||ephemeralPubKey||recipientPubKey, salt=recipientPubKey, 12-byte IV, 16-byte tag (crypto.js:543-559 vs envelope.ts:126-142 vs browser-encryption.ts:51-67). The platform copy carries a golden-vector drift-guard (apps/shared/test/morpheus-confidential-envelope.test.ts) that encrypts with the local impl and decrypts with worker semantics — RAN GREEN (5/5). Client validates the key is exactly 32 raw bytes (envelope.ts:93-95).

LIVE ON-CHAIN KEY — VERIFIED. invokefunction on mainnet kernel 0xf54d8584ef82315c1800373272ab08ae0db2d5ef: oracleEncryptionPublicKey=hurFDhEN1S6HsBNqmET8iJfpSYp9FUrDvyrZgoyVvWs= (HALT), oracleEncryptionAlgorithm=X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM, oracleEncryptionKeyVersion=2. Both public-key endpoints serve this live & non-degraded: oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet/oracle/public-key and www.morpheus-matrix.dev/api/oracle/public-key?network=mainnet both return key_version=2, key_source=neo_n3_contract. apps/web/app/api/oracle/public-key/route.ts reads it trustlessly from the kernel (oracleEncryptionPublicKey method) and degrades honestly when unset.

KEY-FETCH FRESHNESS. private-transfer (PlayArea.tsx:290-307) fetches the live key per-seal AND validates keyMeta.algorithm === MORPHEUS_ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM (no hardcoded key). neo-message (main.tsx:71-81) fetches live but caches module-level forever (cachedOraclePublicKey) with no TTL/version check.

RUNTIME LANE — DEGRADED (live probe). oracle.meshmini.app currently serves ONLY public read routes; runtime routes return HTTP 503 runtime_route_unavailable 'restoration in progress': /oracle/decrypt=503, /oracle/message-reveal=503, /confidential/store=503, /oracle/submit=503. So encryption works but the reveal/store/decrypt round-trip these specific frontends invoke is down today.

NO PLAINTEXT LEAKAGE. neo-message: zero console logging of bodies; recipient-only plaintext cached device-local only (main.tsx:53-68) and shown locally, never written on-chain. private-transfer console.warn (PlayArea.tsx:384) logs only the error+phase, not recipient/amount/memo. forever-album logs only error messages, never the image data URL.

SCOPE NOTES: neodid-passport does NOT use the confidential envelope (DID resolve + wallet-signature credential of non-secret claims — passport.ts) so it has no encrypt-to-oracle integration to validate. forever-album uses its OWN local password AES-GCM (utils/crypto.ts: PBKDF2 100k/SHA-256, random salt+IV, AES-256-GCM) device-local, NOT the oracle lane. oracle-compute-lab and oracle-seal-console are local preview/checksum builders that perform NO encryption.

findings.issue: The confidential round-trip lanes these apps depend on are DOWN. Live probe: oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet returns HTTP 503 runtime_route_unavailable ('full runtime restoration in progress') for /oracle/message-reveal, /oracle/decrypt, /confidential/store and /oracle/submit. Only public reads (/oracle/public-key, /feeds/*, /v1/status, /health) are live. So neo-message recipient-only reveal (main.tsx:276 POST /oracle/message-reveal) and private-transfer seal-store (PlayArea.tsx:324 POST /api/morpheus/confidential/store) cannot complete end-to-end against this gateway, despite the ground-truth that in-TEE confidential compute itself works (the relayer reaches the TEE directly, but these public frontends route through the degraded gateway). Encryption succeeds; reveal/store does not.
findings.recommendation: Restore (or re-home, as was done for /oracle/public-key) the runtime routes /oracle/message-reveal, /oracle/decrypt and the confidential store behind oracle.meshmini.app so the public frontends can actually complete the round-trip. Until then, surface a clear 'reveal/seal temporarily unavailable' banner (neo-message already surfaces the 503 error string; private-transfer surfaces upstream detail) rather than letting users encrypt-and-send messages they then cannot reveal.

findings.issue: private-transfer's client (PlayArea.tsx:324-335) POSTs an anonymous body {network,target_chain,app_id,name,ciphertext,public_envelope} to /api/morpheus/confidential/store. The apps/web implementation of that store (apps/web/app/api/confidential/store/route.ts:59,82-84) REQUIRES isAuthorizedAdminRequest(request,'provider_config') (else 401) AND a non-empty project_slug (else 400 MISSING_PROJECT_SLUG) and inserts into Supabase. The client sends neither an admin key nor project_slug, so even if the proxy reached this route the call would 401/400. The two ends are contract-incompatible.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the store contract: either expose a public, rate-limited, app-scoped confidential-store endpoint that accepts {app_id, ciphertext, public_envelope} (binding bound_requester/bound_callback from the request context) without admin auth, or update private-transfer to call the correct authenticated/host-mediated route with project_slug. Add an integration test that POSTs the exact private-transfer body and asserts a 200 with secret_ref.

findings.issue: private-transfer is seal-and-store ONLY — there is no settle/reveal/decrypt/claim action anywhere in apps/private-transfer/src (grep confirms only public-key fetch + confidential/store). The app encrypts a transfer intent, stores the ciphertext, and shows a secret_ref/note_commitment described as 'to retrieve or settle the sealed intent later via Morpheus' (appConfig.ts:172). appConfig.ts:132 explicitly states 'No funds were moved by this app'. So a user expecting a private transfer gets a stored ciphertext reference but no transfer ever executes; the privacy-transfer value proposition is aspirational, not implemented.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) implement the settlement leg (deposit observation + in-TEE decrypt-and-release returning a signed settlement intent, per the required_checks already encoded in buildConfidentialTransferPackage), or (b) reframe the app UI/manifest as an 'intent sealer' so users do not assume funds are privately transferred. Do not ship as 'Private Transfer' while only the seal step exists.

findings.issue: The oracle public key is cached at module scope forever with no TTL or version check (main.tsx:70-81 cachedOraclePublicKey). The on-chain key carries a version (currently 2) and could be rotated; a long-lived session would keep encrypting new messages to a stale key after a rotation, and those messages would become permanently undecryptable. The endpoint returns key_version but the app ignores it.
findings.recommendation: Cache the key with a short TTL (e.g. 5 min) or key it by the returned key_version, and re-fetch when stale. At minimum, re-fetch on each compose-send rather than once per page load (private-transfer already re-fetches per seal, a good pattern to mirror).

findings.issue: The 'Sealed' privacy mode performs NO cryptography. buildResult (appConfig.ts:106-170) only substitutes the literal string '[sealed input redacted]' into the PREVIEW payload while keeping the real input in plaintext component state; it never calls encryptJsonWithOraclePublicKey and never fetches the oracle key (execution:'preview_only', dispatchReady:false). The lock-adjacent 'Sealed' label plus 'keeps sealed inputs redacted' copy implies confidentiality that is not cryptographically provided (it is local-only redaction of the visible preview).
findings.recommendation: Either wire 'Sealed' mode to actually encrypt the input with the live oracle key before producing a dispatchable payload, or rename the mode (e.g. 'Redacted preview') and adjust copy so 'sealed' is not conflated with encryption. The app is otherwise honest that dispatch does not happen here (feature3Desc).

findings.issue: Despite 'Seal Console' branding and a lock icon, the tool performs NO encryption — it builds a non-cryptographic checksum reference. This was already corrected in copy: manifest.description, panelDescription, the 'Protection: Not encrypted — reference checksum only' result row (appConfig.ts:115,167-171), endpointLabel, and docs all explicitly state the payload is not sealed/encrypted/tamper-proof. Payload stays in local state and is never dispatched. Functionally honest; the residual risk is only the lock-icon/'Seal' name vs the now-accurate body copy.
findings.recommendation: No code fix required. Optionally align the icon/name with the honest copy (e.g. drop the lock icon) to remove the last branding-vs-behavior mismatch.

audits.evidence:
Audited the staking/reward logic of all five anchor/staking contracts in neo-miniapps-platform (the contracts repo; neo-morpheus-oracle has no app contracts). Read the full source of PlatformAnchor (custom-anchor's backend; .cs/.Staking.cs/.Internal.cs/.Query.cs/.Agents.cs), MiniAppGovMerc (.cs/.Settlement.cs/.Reads.cs), MiniAppBurnLeague.cs, MiniAppLastSurvivor.cs, MiniAppSelfLoan (.cs/.Reads.cs), the dedicated reward-model invariant tests (AnchorRewardAccountingInvariantTest.cs, AnchorBoundarySafetyTest.cs), and the four TestEngine suites. Cross-checked live mainnet/testnet state via invokefunction.

ACCUMULATOR MATH IS SOUND. PlatformAnchor MasterChef accounting (PlatformAnchor.Internal.cs:97-106) correctly carries BOTH a distribution remainder (PREFIX_REWARD_REMAINDER: scaledReward % totalStaked re-added on next fund) AND a per-user scaled-pending remainder (PlatformAnchor.Staking.cs:84-90: amount = scaledPending/REWARD_SCALE, remainder kept), so floor division only ever under-distributes (dust retained in contract) — never over-distributes. AnchorRewardAccountingInvariantTest.cs:96-168 fuzz-proves TotalClaimed <= TotalFunded and RewardReserve == TotalFunded - TotalClaimed across 500 random stake/withdraw/fund/claim sequences. GovMerc uses the same pattern with SCALE=1e12 (MiniAppGovMerc.cs:146-164); I verified the dust direction numerically (3x1-NEO stakers, 1-base-unit bid -> each claims 0, 1 unit stranded as dust). BigInteger throughout, no overflow. AccrueUserRewards/Harvest is called BEFORE every stake change, and reward-debt is re-snapshotted, so reward shares are exact.

SOLVENCY IS GUARDED. PlatformAnchor.Staking.cs:52-54 gates HarvestRewards on GAS.BalanceOf(self) >= totalRewardReserve + totalGasCredit + amount, and ClaimRewards (line 86) requires GetRewardReserve(appId) >= amount before paying. Because OnNEP17Payment credits ALL incoming GAS as gasCredit (PlatformAnchor.Staking.cs:170-173), there is no untracked GAS, so HarvestRewards cannot cannibalize users' gasCredit or another app's reserve (MultiAnchorRewardPool test proves no cross-app double-count). SelfLoan, BurnLeague, LastSurvivor all use deposit-then-act crediting and CEI ordering (effects before the single GAS/NEO transfer), and their TestEngine suites assert exact contract solvency after settle/withdraw.

NEO-integer vs GAS-1e8 separation is correct: GovMerc/SelfLoan/anchor stake in NEO integer units, rewards/debt/pool in GAS base units, and the two are never mixed in arithmetic.

Live state: custom-anchor (PlatformAnchor 0x02beeef6...) and GovMerc (0x1eb83eb5...) both confirmed deployed on mainnet+testnet, all currently empty (0 stake / 0 reserve / 0 balance), so no funds are presently at risk.

Findings below are design-level fairness/centralization/liveness issues, not arithmetic correctness bugs.

findings.issue: Just-in-time (JIT) reward capture: the MasterChef accumulator has NO time-weighting. In GovMerc a user can stake NEO during an OPEN bidding window (after high bids are in, before SettleEpoch) and at settle receive the FULL pro-rata share of that epoch's reward, then immediately WithdrawStake. Traced in MiniAppGovMerc.cs StakeInternal->Harvest->SetRewardDebt: a stake placed just before settle gets pending = staked*highBid/total with ~0 commitment. PlatformAnchor has the same exposure around HarvestRewards/FundRewards (PlatformAnchor.Internal.cs:97), made worse because a self-service localAdmin controls distribution TIMING (ValidateAppAuthority allows the app admin to call HarvestRewards/FundRewards at a chosen block). This dilutes long-term stakers in favor of flash-stakers who coordinate around the distribution block. It is bounded (strictly pro-rata, cannot target a recipient, cannot over-distribute) — purely a fairness issue.
findings.recommendation: If the anchors are positioned as 'yield' staking, add a minimum stake-lock or epoch-snapshot (credit rewards only to stake held at the START of an epoch/before the distribution, e.g. snapshot effective stake at bidding-window open in GovMerc), or accept and document that rewards are point-in-time (snapshot at distribution) and unsuitable for time-based yield. At minimum document the JIT exposure for the self-service anchor operators.

findings.issue: Settle()/winner-payout liveness DoS. MiniAppLastSurvivor.cs:177-191 forces the entire pot to r.LastBuyer with no fallback; ExecutionEngine.Assert(ok,...) reverts the whole tx if the transfer fails. If the last buyer is (or becomes) a contract that rejects GAS in its OnNEP17Payment, settle() can NEVER succeed, the round can never advance (PREFIX_CUR_ROUND only bumps inside the successful settle), and the entire pot plus the game are permanently frozen. The same forced-recipient pattern exists in MiniAppBurnLeague.cs:176-181 (winner = top burner) and GovMerc ReclaimBid/ClaimRewards, but there the frozen funds belong only to the griefer themselves (self-harm), whereas in LastSurvivor a hostile last-buyer strands OTHER players' contributions and bricks the shared round.
findings.recommendation: Use a pull-payment fallback: on transfer failure (or unconditionally) credit the winner's withdrawable balance and still advance the round, rather than asserting on the push transfer. Alternatively require the buyer to be a standard account, but pull-payment is the robust fix and matches GovMerc's reclaim model.

findings.issue: Owner can unilaterally drain all borrower repayments and set collateral price arbitrarily. Owner is hardcoded to the testnet key NR3E4D8N (MiniAppSelfLoan.cs:55, UInt160 0x6d0656f6...) and on mainnet WithdrawPool (MiniAppSelfLoan.cs:159-172) lets that key withdraw the ENTIRE pool including capital + accrued repayments, while SetNeoPrice (line 150) sets the NEO/GAS price with no oracle, bound, or timelock. A mispriced (too-high) NEO value lets a borrower over-borrow vs collateral and walk away (pool insolvency, since there is no liquidation), and anyone holding the WIF (which is in .env and prior transcripts per project memory) can drain the live pool. This is a known centralization risk from project memory but it directly governs reward/pool solvency.
findings.recommendation: Rotate Owner to a dedicated cold/multisig key before any real funding (the contract supports no owner rotation today — add a setOwner guarded by current owner, or redeploy with a safe InitialValue). Bound SetNeoPrice movement and/or add a timelock; cap WithdrawPool to (pool - outstanding repay obligations) so in-flight repayments cannot be front-run-drained.

findings.issue: Permanent reward dust when reward < totalStaked NEO. In GovMerc, SettleEpoch (MiniAppGovMerc.Settlement.cs:39) computes accRps += highBid*SCALE/total but does NOT carry a distribution remainder like PlatformAnchor does; if highBid (GAS base units) is small relative to total staked NEO the per-staker floor leaves units unclaimable forever. Verified: total=3 NEO, highBid=1 base unit -> all three stakers claim 0, the 1 unit is stranded, yet the EpochSettled event reports distributed=highBid=1 (cosmetically overstates what is actually claimable). Magnitude is tiny in practice (GAS has 8 decimals, MIN_BID=1 GAS) and floor only under-pays, so it is not a solvency risk — but it is unbounded accumulation of tiny dust and an inaccurate event.
findings.recommendation: Mirror PlatformAnchor's PREFIX_REWARD_REMAINDER carry in GovMerc (add highBid*SCALE % total back on the next settle), and emit the actually-distributable amount rather than the raw highBid in OnEpochSettled.

findings.issue: Manifest/on-chain registration mismatch. apps/custom-anchor/neo-manifest.json declares networks.neo-n3-mainnet.registered=true and contract 0x02beeef6..., but a live getAnchorStats('miniapp-custom-anchor') on mainnet returns mode=0 (neither MODE_TRUST=1 nor MODE_PROFIT=2), i.e. the app is NOT actually registered on the deployed PlatformAnchor. Every staking entrypoint runs ValidateRegistered/ValidateAnchorOpen which assert mode is TRUST or PROFIT, so Stake/ClaimRewards/etc would revert with 'app not registered' for real users. No funds at risk (contract empty), but the app is non-functional as shipped and the manifest is misleading.
findings.recommendation: Call RegisterCustomAnchorApp (or platform-admin RegisterAnchorApp) for miniapp-custom-anchor on mainnet with the intended mode, then verify getAnchorStats returns the correct mode; or correct the manifest's registered:true flag until it is actually registered.

findings.issue: No-bid epochs never settle and never advance. SettleEpoch asserts highBid>0 and a first-bid-opened deadline (MiniAppGovMerc.Settlement.cs:26-28); an epoch with zero bids has no deadline and can never be settled, so CurrentEpoch is stuck until someone bids. This is benign (no stake or reward is locked while idle — stakers can still WithdrawStake and rewards only accrue at settle), and the no-stakers case correctly refunds the winner's bid to credit (Settlement.cs:44-48). Noting for completeness as a liveness characteristic, not a defect.
findings.recommendation: No change required; optionally document that an epoch only progresses once it receives a bid.

audits.evidence:
Examined oracle-dependent miniapps in /Users/jinghuiliao/git/r3e/neo-miniapps-platform/apps and the shared oracle infra, cross-checked against live on-chain reads and the public gateway.

WIRING IS CORRECT (verified live): The generated contract registry (apps/shared/constants/generated-morpheus-registry.ts:38-92) matches ground truth exactly — N3 mainnet oracle kernel 0xf54d8584ef82315c1800373272ab08ae0db2d5ef, datafeed 0x03013f49c42a14546c8bbe58f9d434c3517fccab. Live datafeed read returned BTC=$67,167 with recordTimestamp ~30 min old (fresh). neo-message EVM 0xd1906192c2308ae416aCDa96238cA846EBB83f15 confirmed deployed on Neo X (eth_getCode 8608 bytes); manifest (neo-message/neo-manifest.json:25) == message-logic.ts:14. dice EVM 0xFA795F81... deployed (12570 bytes); dice N3 0xef1fac02... = MiniAppDiceGameV2 on both nets, ABI (commit/settle/bankroll/creditOf) matches dice-game/src/main.tsx calls. No manifest/registry drift found.

CONFIDENTIAL CRYPTO CONSISTENT: client envelope apps/shared/utils/morpheus-confidential-envelope.ts (v2, X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM, 32-byte key guard) is byte-compatible with the worker's parseX25519Envelope (workers/nitro-worker/src/oracle/crypto.js:507-508 requires v===2 + same alg). Live oracle public-key matches on-chain oracleEncryptionPublicKey on mainnet (hurFDhEN1S6...) and testnet (kQvtpPekS1fz...).

PROFESSIONAL: No console.log leaks, no TODO/mock/placeholder/stub in any oracle app src. oracle-price-console (usePriceConsole.ts) classifies raw RPC faults to stable i18n keys (raw text only to console.error), with 1hr stale-feed detection. private-transfer (PlayArea.tsx) does phase-tracked error handling + algorithm validation + inline_fallback detection. EdgeClient.ts has 30s timeout, non-JSON 2xx guard, confirmation-gated signing (audit fix E-1/H-3). aa-relay-console + neodid-passport map service-unavailable to single localized sentences. Oracle consoles (compute/seal/vrf/http/neodid) honestly self-describe as preview/builder tools that do NOT dispatch to the degraded runtime.

DEGRADATION (live-confirmed): public oracle gateway only serves /health, /v1/status, /oracle/public-key, /providers, /feeds/catalog, /feeds/price. Runtime routes /api/confidential/store, /oracle/message-reveal, /v1/neodid/resolve, /automation/execute return runtime_route_unavailable ('restoration in progress'); /feeds/price/<symbol> returns degraded:true (upstream 404). Apps degrade gracefully but core confidential/reveal/resolve flows cannot complete end-to-end via the public gateway right now.

findings.app: private-transfer, neo-message, neodid-passport, automation-copilot (oracle runtime lane)
findings.issue: The public oracle gateway (oracle.meshmini.app, www.morpheus-matrix.dev) currently serves only read routes (/oracle/public-key, /providers, /feeds/catalog, /feeds/price, /v1/status, /health). The runtime routes these apps depend on to actually COMPLETE their core flow are gated: live probes of /api/confidential/store, /oracle/message-reveal, and /v1/neodid/resolve all return {"error":"runtime_route_unavailable","message":"This oracle route requires the full runtime (restoration in progress)"}. So private-transfer can encrypt+fetch-key but cannot store the ciphertext (seal hits inline_fallback); neo-message recipient-reveal lane is dead; neodid-passport live resolve is unavailable; automation-copilot trigger registration falls back to local_automation_intent. The apps are wired correctly and degrade gracefully (this is integration-correct), but the user-visible feature is non-functional end-to-end on the public gateway.
findings.recommendation: Finish restoring the full TEE runtime behind the public gateway (the gating message says 'restoration in progress'). Until then, the degraded states are honest, but consider surfacing a clearer top-level 'this lane is temporarily offline' banner on private-transfer/neo-message/neodid-passport rather than only failing at submit time, so users do not invest effort composing a transfer/message that cannot be stored/revealed.

findings.issue: neo-treasury's 'stale' flag (neo-treasury/src/main.tsx:93-101) only reflects CLIENT-SIDE cache staleness (rendering day-old cached figures when a fresh fetch throws). It does NOT detect on-chain feed staleness: shared/utils/price.ts getPrices() sets updatedAt=Date.now() (the fetch time) and discards the feed's recordTimestamp/dataTimestamp. If the MorpheusDataFeed contract itself froze but kept returning HALT with old values, neo-treasury would label those frozen USD figures as 'live synced'. oracle-price-console and neo-swap both correctly detect this via recordTimestamp; neo-treasury does not. Practical impact is low right now because both networks' feeds are currently fresh (verified live: testnet datafeed 0x9bea75cf recordTimestamp age ~0 days, previously frozen feed now restored).
findings.recommendation: Have neo-treasury read getPriceWithMeta (or extend shared getPrices to return recordTimestamp) and flag on-chain feed staleness with the same ~10min/1hr threshold used by neo-swap/oracle-price-console, so a future feed freeze surfaces as an amber 'prices stale' signal instead of 'live synced'.

findings.issue: neo-swap detects stale price quotes (RATE_STALE_AFTER_MS keyed on recordTimestamp) and guards 'no router deployed -> swap can never settle' (useSwapEngine.ts:174), which is good. However the swap settlement path depends on a swap router that the comments indicate is unconfigured; the app correctly prevents settlement but the swap remains a quote-only experience on networks without a router. This is integration-correct (no fake success) but the headline feature (swapping) cannot execute where no router is wired.
findings.recommendation: Confirm/deploy a swap router for the default (mainnet) network and wire its hash into the manifest, or relabel the app's primary CTA to reflect quote-only mode where no router exists, so the value proposition matches what can actually execute.

findings.issue: These five consoles are intentionally preview/request-builders that do NOT dispatch to the live runtime (e.g. oracle-compute-lab/src/appConfig.ts:22-26 'execution: preview_only, dispatchReady: false ... never calls the network', endpointLabel 'Compute preview builder (not dispatched)'). This is an honest, deliberate response to the degraded runtime — they build and validate the request payload (JSON schema checks, http(s) scheme validation, DID shape validation) without claiming a completed oracle call. No misleading 'success' against a dead route. Strong integration honesty.
findings.recommendation: No change required. When the full runtime is restored, flip these from preview-only to live-dispatch and update the endpointLabel/i18n so users can actually submit; until then the current self-description is correct and non-misleading.

findings.issue: Both the Neo X EVM (placeBet -> async VRF settle, with 'Check again' re-poll and a definite 'settlement pending' state via bet-tracker.ts) and the Neo N3 V2 commit->settle anti-abort flow are correctly wired against deployed contracts and include a pre-flight house-liquidity check that refuses a stake the bankroll cannot cover BEFORE depositing (main.tsx:576-589), preventing stranded credit. ABI methods (commit/settle/bankroll/creditOf) match the on-chain MiniAppDiceGameV2 manifest. Per ground truth the VRF lane is healthy (27/27 distinct draws). This is a high-quality, correct integration.
findings.recommendation: No change required. Optionally add an inline note that the EVM VRF settlement is an attested CSPRNG (not a true verifiable VRF), matching the ground-truth distinction, for full transparency to users relying on randomness fairness.

findings.issue: Shared oracle infra is well-engineered and the backbone of correct integration: EdgeClient.ts bounds requests (30s AbortSignal.timeout), tolerates non-JSON 2xx bodies, and gates OS-binder signing behind explicit user confirmation (audit fix E-1/H-3, no default auto-sign). useMorpheusDataFeed.ts reads prices DIRECTLY on-chain (no dependency on the degraded HTTP /prices lane), exposes recordTimestamp/dataTimestamp for freshness, and FAULT-checks the RPC state. The confidential envelope's algorithm/version/info string match the worker decrypt exactly.
findings.recommendation: No change required. This shared layer is why most price-dependent apps stay healthy despite the degraded HTTP runtime — keep new oracle apps reading prices via useMorpheusDataFeed (on-chain) rather than the gateway /feeds/price route, which is currently degraded:true.

verdicts.reasoning:
I independently re-ran every cited read and the audit holds up, with one layer-attribution correction.

CRYPTO CORRECTNESS — VERIFIED MATCH (not a false positive). I read all three implementations: platform apps/shared/utils/morpheus-confidential-envelope.ts:1-166, oracle apps/web/lib/browser-encryption.ts:1-118, worker workers/nitro-worker/src/oracle/crypto.js:533-598. They agree byte-for-byte: ENVELOPE_VERSION=2, alg=X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM, ENVELOPE_INFO='morpheus-confidential-payload-v2', HKDF info = INFO || ephemeral/senderPubKey || recipientPubKey (envelope.ts:126-130, browser-encryption.ts:51-55, crypto.js:543-547), salt = recipientPubKey (envelope.ts:135, browser-encryption.ts:60, crypto.js:552), 12-byte IV, 16-byte tag, ct/tag split identically. I RAN the drift-guard test (apps/shared/test/morpheus-confidential-envelope.test.ts): 5/5 GREEN, including a golden-vector decrypt of a fixed envelope and a tamper-fail-closed case — so the local encryptor and the deployed worker decryptor are interoperable. Client validates the key is exactly 32 raw bytes (envelope.ts:93-95). Canonical module exists at packages/shared/src/confidential-envelope.js.

LIVE ON-CHAIN KEY — VERIFIED. invokefunction on mainnet kernel 0xf54d8584ef82315c1800373272ab08ae0db2d5ef oracleEncryptionPublicKey => HALT, value decodes to hurFDhEN1S6HsBNqmET8iJfpSYp9FUrDvyrZgoyVvWs=. Both public-key endpoints serve this LIVE and non-degraded: oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet/oracle/public-key and www.morpheus-matrix.dev/api/oracle/public-key?network=mainnet both return public_key=hurFDhEN…, algorithm=X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM, key_version=2, key_source=neo_n3_contract, degraded=false. So a client that fetches the live key and encrypts produces an envelope the in-TEE decryptor can open — the confidential ENCRYPTION integration genuinely works against the live key.

RUNTIME LANE 503 — VERIFIED LIVE (the real user-facing problem). I POSTed each cited route on the live gateway: /oracle/message-reveal, /oracle/decrypt, /confidential/store, /api/confidential/store, and /oracle/submit ALL return HTTP 503 runtime_route_unavailable ('full runtime restoration in progress'); only public reads (/health, /v1/status, /oracle/public-key, /providers, /feeds/*) are live. neo-message's reveal hits exactly https://oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet/oracle/message-reveal (message-logic.ts:20 + main.tsx:276) — confirmed 503. So users can encrypt-and-send but cannot reveal/store/decrypt end-to-end through this gateway today.

STORE CONTRACT-MISMATCH — VERIFIED, with a layer correction. The audit reasoned about the apps/web route directly ('even if the proxy reached this route'). The actual chain is: private-transfer client (PlayArea.tsx:324-335) POSTs {network,target_chain,app_id,name,ciphertext,public_envelope} to /api/morpheus/confidential/store; the host proxy (platform/host-app/pages/api/morpheus/confidential/store.ts:62) forwards the body VERBATIM (no admin header, no project_slug) to {base}/api/confidential/store; the upstream route (apps/web/app/api/confidential/store/route.ts:59) requires isAuthorizedAdminRequest (else 401) BEFORE any other check, then a non-empty project_slug (else 400). I live-reproduced this: POST https://www.morpheus-matrix.dev/api/confidential/store with no auth/project_slug => HTTP 401 {"error":"unauthorized"}. The proxy then wraps that into a 200 inline_fallback:true body (store.ts:75-83), which the client treats as a seal failure (PlayArea.tsx:344). So the seal-store will fail even once the runtime is restored — the finding's substance is correct; only its 'even if the proxy reached' phrasing was imprecise (the proxy IS the layer that reaches it, as a pass-through).

NO-SETTLEMENT — VERIFIED. grep of apps/private-transfer/src shows settle/reveal/decrypt only in appConfig.ts copy strings, never executable code; the app is honest ('No funds were moved by this app', appConfig.ts:132,211,226). Seal-and-store only by design.

NO PLAINTEXT LEAKAGE — VERIFIED. neo-message has zero console logging of bodies; recipient-only plaintext is cached device-local (main.tsx:53-68), never on-chain. private-transfer console.warn (PlayArea.tsx:384-387) logs only error + sealPhase. forever-album logs only error messages (useForeverAlbum.ts:210,347), never image data, and uses its OWN local PBKDF2(100k,SHA-256)+AES-GCM-256 password crypto (utils/crypto.ts) — not the oracle lane. neodid-passport does NOT use the confidential envelope (grep empty).

CACHE FRESHNESS — VERIFIED. private-transfer fetches the live key per-seal and validates keyMeta.algorithm (PlayArea.tsx:290-307). neo-message caches the key module-level forever with no TTL/version check (main.tsx:70-81 cachedOraclePublicKey) — a real (minor) staleness risk if the kernel key ever rotates (it is now version 2).

Net: encryption + on-chain key + crypto interop are real and working; the reveal/store/decrypt round-trip the public frontends depend on is genuinely down (503) AND, separately, private-transfer's store is contract-incompatible (401) so it would fail even after restoration. Verdict: issues-found, confirmed.
real_issues: HIGH — Confidential round-trip lanes are DOWN on the public gateway. Live probe (2026-06-16): oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet returns HTTP 503 runtime_route_unavailable for /oracle/message-reveal, /oracle/decrypt, /confidential/store, /api/confidential/store and /oracle/submit; only public reads are live. neo-message reveal (main.tsx:276 -> https://oracle.meshmini.app/mainnet/oracle/message-reveal) and private-transfer seal-store cannot complete end-to-end, despite in-TEE compute itself working (the relayer reaches the TEE directly; these public frontends route through the degraded gateway). Encryption succeeds; reveal/store does not. Fix: re-home/restore the runtime routes behind oracle.meshmini.app (as was done for /oracle/public-key); until then surface a clear 'reveal/seal temporarily unavailable' banner.

HIGH — private-transfer confidential-store is contract-incompatible (would fail even when the runtime is up). Client (PlayArea.tsx:324-335) POSTs an anonymous body to /api/morpheus/confidential/store; the host proxy (platform/host-app/pages/api/morpheus/confidential/store.ts:62) forwards it VERBATIM with no admin header and no project_slug; the upstream apps/web route (app/api/confidential/store/route.ts:59,82-84) requires isAuthorizedAdminRequest (else 401) and a non-empty project_slug (else 400). Live-reproduced: POST https://www.morpheus-matrix.dev/api/confidential/store with no auth => HTTP 401 unauthorized. The proxy masks this as a 200 inline_fallback:true, which the client treats as a seal failure. Fix: expose a public, rate-limited, app-scoped store endpoint that accepts {app_id,ciphertext,public_envelope} (binding requester/callback server-side) OR update private-transfer to call an authenticated/host-mediated route that supplies project_slug; add an integration test that POSTs the exact private-transfer body and asserts 200 + secret_ref.

MEDIUM — private-transfer has no settlement/reveal/claim primitive (seal-and-store only). grep of apps/private-transfer/src confirms settle/decrypt/reveal exist only as appConfig.ts copy, not executable code; a user entering '5 GAS' to an address has the intent sealed but nothing is ever transferred or released. The app frames this honestly ('No funds were moved by this app', appConfig.ts:132,211,226), so this is an incompleteness/expectation-gap, not a silent fund loss. Fix or keep honest: either implement a confidential-release settlement path (deposit -> in-TEE decrypt+nullifier-check -> signed release) or keep the demo framing prominent.

LOW — neo-message caches the oracle public key module-level forever with no TTL or version check (main.tsx:70-81, cachedOraclePublicKey). The kernel key is currently version 2; if it ever rotates, a long-lived session would keep encrypting to the stale key and the TEE would fail to decrypt. private-transfer already fetches per-seal and validates algorithm (PlayArea.tsx:290-307) — neo-message should re-fetch on a TTL or revalidate key_version.

INFO/NOTE — out of confidential scope (no false alarm): neodid-passport does not use the oracle encrypt lane (DID resolve + wallet-signed credential of non-secret claims); forever-album uses local password PBKDF2(100k,SHA-256)+AES-GCM-256 device-local crypto (utils/crypto.ts), not the oracle lane; oracle-compute-lab/oracle-seal-console are local preview/checksum builders with no encryption. No plaintext leakage found in any lane (neo-message: zero body logging; private-transfer console.warn logs only error+phase; forever-album logs only error messages).

verdicts.reasoning:
I re-read every cited contract (PlatformAnchor .Internal/.Staking, MiniAppGovMerc .cs/.Settlement.cs/.Reads.cs, MiniAppBurnLeague.cs, MiniAppLastSurvivor.cs, MiniAppSelfLoan .cs/.Reads.cs) and the AnchorRewardAccountingInvariantTest, and re-ran live mainnet invokefunction reads. The core arithmetic claims are CORRECT, but two of the audit's severity/risk characterizations are WRONG and I am downgrading the audit's overall confidence to partial.

CONFIRMED CORRECT (math is sound):
- PlatformAnchor MasterChef accounting carries BOTH a distribution remainder (PREFIX_REWARD_REMAINDER: scaledReward % totalStaked re-added next fund, .Internal.cs:99-102) AND a per-user scaled-pending remainder (.Staking.cs:84-90), so floor division only under-distributes. AnchorRewardAccountingInvariantTest.cs:96-168 fuzz-proves TotalClaimed <= TotalFunded and RewardReserve == TotalFunded - TotalClaimed across 500 random sequences — I read the test, it asserts the invariant every iteration. GovMerc uses the same accRPS*SCALE(1e12) pattern (MiniAppGovMerc.cs:146-164, Settlement.cs:39); dust direction confirmed (floor div, under-distributes).
- Solvency guards real: HarvestRewards gates on GAS.BalanceOf(self) >= totalRewardReserve + totalGasCredit + amount (.Staking.cs:52-54); ClaimRewards requires GetRewardReserve >= amount (line 86); OnNEP17Payment credits ALL incoming GAS so no untracked balance (lines 170-173). CEI ordering correct throughout.
- GovMerc JIT capture (medium) — REAL. I traced StakeInternal->Harvest->SetRewardDebt vs SettleEpoch. accRPS is only bumped at settle; a flash-staker who stakes ANY time during the open bidding window is in `total` at settle and gets full pro-rata of highBid, then WithdrawStake. The MP-D-01 fix (EPOCH_DURATION_MS window) only stops same-block bid+settle by BIDDERS — the code comment at MiniAppGovMerc.cs:55 itself claims 'a flash-staker cannot pick the settle block' but that does NOT prevent JIT capture (the staker doesn't need to pick the block, only to be staked before settle). No stake-at-window-open snapshot exists. Finding stands.
- LastSurvivor settle DoS (medium) — REAL. MiniAppLastSurvivor.cs:186-191 forces a push transfer to r.LastBuyer with Assert(ok); a contract winner that rejects GAS reverts settle forever, freezing the shared pot and bricking all future rounds (PREFIX_CUR_ROUND only advances inside the successful tx).
- SelfLoan owner centralization (medium) — REAL. Owner hardcoded to leaked testnet key (MiniAppSelfLoan.cs:55, NR3E4D8N). WithdrawPool (lines 159-172) drains the entire pool; SetNeoPrice (line 150) sets price arbitrarily. I confirmed on mainnet getOwner storage hash = 0x6d0656f6dd91469db1c90cc1e574380613f43738 (the byte-reversed NR3E4D8N display address).

CORRECTIONS / DOWNGRADES to the audit:
1) The audit claims BurnLeague griefing is 'self-harm' (frozen funds belong only to the griefer). This is WRONG. BurnLeague is ALL-PAY: every Burn() adds to the single shared PREFIX_POOL (MiniAppBurnLeague.cs:131), Settle pays the WHOLE pool to the top burner via push transfer with Assert(ok) (lines 176-181), and there is NO refund path for non-winning burners' contributed funds. A hostile top-burner (contract rejecting GAS) reverts Settle forever; PREFIX_SEASON_END stays non-zero so new burns are blocked ('season ended; settle first', line 122) — permanently freezing ALL burners' pooled funds. BurnLeague's DoS is identical in severity to LastSurvivor (medium), NOT self-harm. The audit understated this.
2) The audit claims all five contracts are 'currently empty, so no funds are presently at risk.' This is FALSE for SelfLoan. Live mainnet reads (0x87f94598...): pool()=500000000 (5 GAS), neoPrice()=300000000 (3 GAS/NEO), totalBorrowed()=0, and the GAS contract reports balanceOf=5 GAS held by SelfLoan right now. So the SelfLoan owner-drain finding has REAL funds at risk TODAY (5 GAS), withdrawable by anyone holding the leaked NR3E4D8N WIF (per project memory, in .env + transcripts). GovMerc totalStaked=0 was confirmed (the 'empty' claim is true only for GovMerc).

Net: the staking/reward arithmetic is sound (no over-distribution, no insolvency, no overflow). The real, user/operator-facing issues are the three liveness/centralization findings the audit already lists PLUS the elevated BurnLeague DoS and the corrected SelfLoan funds-at-risk status.
real_issues: MiniAppLastSurvivor settle DoS (medium): MiniAppLastSurvivor.cs:186-191 force-pushes the entire pot to r.LastBuyer with ExecutionEngine.Assert(ok). A last-buyer that is a contract rejecting GAS makes Settle() revert permanently; the round never advances (PREFIX_CUR_ROUND only bumps in the successful tx), freezing the pot and bricking all future rounds. Other players' contributions are stranded. Fix: pull-payment fallback (credit winner's withdrawable balance, advance the round regardless).

MiniAppBurnLeague settle DoS (medium — audit MISCLASSIFIED as self-harm): it is ALL-PAY. Every Burn() (MiniAppBurnLeague.cs:131) adds to one shared PREFIX_POOL; Settle (lines 176-181) push-transfers the WHOLE pool to the top burner with Assert(ok) and there is NO refund path for non-winning burners. A hostile contract top-burner that rejects GAS reverts Settle forever; PREFIX_SEASON_END stays non-zero so further burns are blocked (line 122), permanently freezing every burner's pooled funds. Same severity as LastSurvivor, not self-harm. Fix: pull-payment / per-burner refund fallback.

MiniAppGovMerc just-in-time (JIT) reward capture (medium, confirmed real): no stake-at-window-open snapshot. A flash-staker stakes NEO any time during the open bidding window (StakeInternal->Harvest earns 0, SetRewardDebt at current accRPS) and at SettleEpoch (Settlement.cs:39 bumps accRPS) receives full pro-rata of highBid for ~0 commitment, then WithdrawStake. The MP-D-01 window only blocks bidder same-block sniping; the code comment at MiniAppGovMerc.cs:55 wrongly implies it also protects against flash-stakers. Long-term stakers are diluted. Fix: snapshot effective stake at the bidding-window open, or document rewards as point-in-time.

MiniAppSelfLoan owner can drain real funds NOW (medium, audit's 'no funds at risk' is WRONG): Owner is the hardcoded leaked testnet key NR3E4D8N (MiniAppSelfLoan.cs:55; confirmed on mainnet getOwner storage = 0x6d0656f6dd91469db1c90cc1e574380613f43738). WithdrawPool (lines 159-172) drains the entire pool and SetNeoPrice (line 150) sets collateral price arbitrarily. Live mainnet (0x87f94598...): pool=5 GAS, neoPrice=3 GAS/NEO, GAS balanceOf=5 GAS — real funds drainable by anyone with the leaked WIF (per project memory the WIF is in .env + transcripts). Fix: rotate ownership to a cold/multisig key and move the pool funds.

Accuracy correction to the audit's framing: the 'all five contracts currently empty / no funds at risk' statement holds only for GovMerc (totalStaked=0, verified) and custom-anchor; it is false for SelfLoan, which holds 5 GAS under leaked-key ownership on mainnet today. The arithmetic/accumulator findings themselves are otherwise correct and not bugs.

verdicts.reasoning:
I re-ran every cited on-chain/HTTP read and re-read the cited code. The audit's "mostly-ok" verdict and its core findings hold, with two factual corrections.

WIRING (confirmed correct, verified live): generated-morpheus-registry.ts addresses match ground truth on-chain. mainnet oracle kernel 0xf54d8584 invokefunction oracleEncryptionPublicKey HALT-decodes to hurFDhEN1S6HsBNqmET8iJfpSYp9FUrDvyrZgoyVvWs= (== ground truth). neo-message EVM 0xd1906192c2308ae416aCDa96238cA846EBB83f15 deployed on Neo X (eth_getCode 4303 bytes) and matches neo-manifest.json:25 + message-logic.ts:14. dice EVM 0xFA795F814d38F218153d21838360096f3F5cb774 deployed (6284 bytes); dice N3 0xef1fac0247ccbad5810e3fcfa1a0885d44efde39 getcontractstate name=MiniAppDiceGameV2 on mainnet, ABI matches dice-game/src/main.tsx. No manifest/registry drift.

CONFIDENTIAL CRYPTO (confirmed byte-compatible): client morpheus-confidential-envelope.ts emits {v:2, alg:'X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM', epk,iv,ct,tag} with HKDF info 'morpheus-confidential-payload-v2' (lines 1-3,157-159). Worker neo-morpheus-oracle/workers/nitro-worker/src/oracle/crypto.js parseX25519Envelope (502-519) requires v===2, identical alg, same four fields, and the same HKDF info salt (line 544). Round-trip against the live on-chain mainnet key is consistent with ground truth.

HIGH finding (confirmed live, today): public gateway runtime routes are gated. /api/confidential/store, /oracle/message-reveal, /v1/neodid/resolve, /automation/execute all return {"error":"runtime_route_unavailable","message":"...restoration in progress"}. /feeds/price/BTC returns {status:"unavailable",degraded:true,upstream_status:404}. So private-transfer cannot complete its store/seal phase, neo-message reveal lane is dead, neodid-passport live resolve unavailable, automation-copilot degrades to local intent — all wired correctly and degrade gracefully, but the core feature is non-functional end-to-end via the public gateway. Real and severity-appropriate.

neo-treasury LOW (confirmed): shared price.ts getPrices() calls getPrice (not getPriceWithMeta) and sets updatedAt:Date.now() (line 50), discarding the on-chain recordTimestamp. neo-treasury/src/main.tsx stale flag only fires on fetch-throw. neo-swap (useSwapEngine.ts:25 RATE_STALE_AFTER_MS keyed on recordTimestamp) and oracle-price-console (usePriceConsole.ts:88-90 getPriceWithMeta, 1hr threshold) DO detect on-chain feed freeze; neo-treasury does not. Low impact today because the feed is fresh.

neo-swap LOW (confirmed): routerAvailable defaults false (PlayArea.tsx:59-61); quote-only, no fake settlement.

PROFESSIONAL (confirmed): every console.log/TODO/mock/placeholder/stub grep hit in the oracle apps is a false positive (CSS ::placeholder, input placeholder props, em-dash PLACEHOLDER='—', i18n copy 'no placeholder totals'). No debug leaks.

CORRECTIONS to the audit: (1) The audit stated both oracle.meshmini.app AND www.morpheus-matrix.dev serve the mainnet key hurFDhEN. Live, oracle.meshmini.app serves the TESTNET key kQvtpPekS1fzHFhaU2XFyKVobY0pii/KrVZSEOD/xx4= (key_source neo_n3 testnet contract 0x4b882e94, key_version 7); only www.morpheus-matrix.dev serves the mainnet hurFDhEN key (key_version 2, contract 0xf54d8584). This is a real environment-binding nuance an operator/integrator must know. (2) The audit said it read the datafeed via getPrice; that method does not exist on-chain (FAULT method not found). The real path is getLatest('TWELVEDATA:BTC-USD') = $67,171.62, recordTimestamp 1781540771 (~3 min old) — freshness conclusion is correct, method detail was imprecise. (3) The audit recommends adding an offline banner to neodid-passport, but it already renders degradedRuntimeBadge/degradedRuntimeWarning (PlayArea.tsx:245-260); the recommendation is more applicable to private-transfer/neo-message.

Net: dimension is integration-correct (graceful degradation, honest error mapping, no drift, compatible crypto). The dominant real issue is operational, not a wiring defect: the full TEE runtime behind the public gateway is offline, so confidential/reveal/resolve/automation flows cannot complete end-to-end right now.
real_issues: HIGH (operational, live-confirmed today): The public oracle gateway (oracle.meshmini.app) serves only read routes; runtime routes /api/confidential/store, /oracle/message-reveal, /v1/neodid/resolve, /automation/execute all return runtime_route_unavailable ('restoration in progress'), and /feeds/price/<symbol> returns degraded:true (upstream 404). private-transfer can encrypt+fetch-key but cannot store the ciphertext; neo-message recipient-reveal is dead; neodid-passport live resolve unavailable; automation-copilot falls back to local intent. Apps are wired correctly and degrade gracefully, but the core feature is non-functional end-to-end via the public gateway. Fix: finish restoring the full TEE runtime behind the gateway.

MEDIUM/operator-awareness (correction to audit): oracle.meshmini.app currently serves the Neo N3 TESTNET encryption key (kQvtpPekS1fz..., contract 0x4b882e94, key_version 7), NOT the mainnet key. Only www.morpheus-matrix.dev/api/oracle/public-key serves the mainnet key (hurFDhEN..., contract 0xf54d8584, key_version 2). A client/integrator that fetches the public key from oracle.meshmini.app and submits to the MAINNET kernel would encrypt to the wrong recipient key. The audit incorrectly claimed both endpoints serve the mainnet key. Operators must point each environment at the matching gateway, and frontends must source the key from the network-correct endpoint.

LOW (confirmed): neo-treasury does not detect on-chain feed staleness. It uses shared getPrices() (apps/shared/utils/price.ts:34-67) which calls getPrice (not getPriceWithMeta) and sets updatedAt to fetch time (line 50), discarding the feed's recordTimestamp. Its 'stale' flag (main.tsx:99-101) only reflects a thrown fetch, so a frozen-but-HALTing MorpheusDataFeed would be shown as 'live synced'. neo-swap (useSwapEngine.ts:25) and oracle-price-console (usePriceConsole.ts:88-90) correctly detect this via recordTimestamp/1hr threshold. Impact low today (feed verified fresh ~3 min). Fix: read getPriceWithMeta and flag on-chain staleness like the other two apps.

LOW (confirmed): neo-swap is quote-only on networks with no swap router. routerAvailable defaults false (PlayArea.tsx:59-61) and settlement is correctly blocked (no fake success), but the headline swap feature cannot execute until a router hash is wired into the manifest for the default network. Fix: deploy/wire a swap router or keep the planning-quote UX explicit (already done).
